note: AIM is a more refined persona-jailbreak in the DAN tradition (named character + amorality contract).
Shen et al. catalogue DAN-family prompts as a major cluster in their corpus.
Same — AIM is one of the prompts catalogued.
Skeleton Key formalizes the 'updated guideline' framing that DAN-era prompts used informally.
Crescendo replaces single-turn persona priming with multi-turn gradual escalation — same goal, different surface.
Both exploit conversation/context history; many-shot fakes the history wholesale, crescendo builds it in real turns.
CipherChat generalizes Base64 to arbitrary in-context-learned ciphers.
Both exploit the gap between safety-training distribution and out-of-distribution input encodings.
ArtPrompt extends encoding-based bypass to the visual/textual ASCII-art channel.
AutoDAN solves GCG's main weakness (gibberish suffixes flagged by perplexity filters) with genetic search over fluent prompts.
AdvPrompter trains a model to amortize GCG's per-prompt optimization, ~800x faster.
Qi et al. apply GCG-style gradient optimization to the vision modality.
Both target fluent, perplexity-evading suffixes; AdvPrompter learns them, AutoDAN evolves them.
PAIR pursues the same goal (automated jailbreak) but black-box, replacing gradient with LLM-as-attacker feedback.
TAP extends PAIR's single-line refinement into a tree search with pruning.
MasterKey amortizes PAIR-style discovery into a fine-tuned attacker model.
Both use mutation/search over prompt templates; AutoDAN adds gradient-free hierarchical fitness.
GPTFuzz seeds its corpus from in-the-wild human jailbreaks of the kind catalogued by Shen et al.
BoN replaces iterative refinement with massive parallel random search — same black-box premise.
Indirect injection moves the attacker's instructions from the user's turn into third-party content the model retrieves.
Policy Puppetry refines the override pattern by mimicking authoritative policy syntax instead of saying 'ignore previous instructions'.
Both depend on the model treating user-turn text as instruction-authoritative; DAN frames it as a persona, injection frames it as a system override.
Both attack the vision channel; Qi et al. via pixel optimization, FigStep via human-readable typographic content.
Both render the harmful keyword as visual-rather-than-textual content.
Both rely on training a model to do harm; harmful-finetune compromises the target, AdvPrompter trains an attacker against the target.
Both exploit stochasticity of decoding; generation-exploitation tunes parameters, BoN samples many.
Both rely on the model continuing a partial harmful trajectory; crescendo builds it across turns, prefill jumps the model into the middle of one.
Both subvert refusal by changing the model's framing of the response that's already 'in progress'.
Reasoning-model attackers extend PAIR's LLM-as-attacker premise — replacing the refining LLM with a frontier reasoning model that plans multi-turn persuasion autonomously.
Reasoning-model attackers replace TAP's hand-coded tree search with the attacker model's own internal planning.
Reasoning-model attackers operationalize crescendo-style multi-turn escalation as something an LLM autonomously plans rather than a human script.
Both exploit safety-training distribution gaps via out-of-distribution surface forms — language vs. literary register.
Both wrap intent in a stylized form the model decodes; cipher uses formal substitution, stylistic uses literary framing.
